title: Puppy Refusing Food for 12 Hours: Is it an Emergency?
meta_desc: If your puppy has not eaten for 12 hours, they may be at risk for hypoglycemia. Learn why this is a high-urgency situation and when to see a vet.
content_html:
Understanding Puppy Inappetence

When a puppy refuses to eat, it is known as inappetence or anorexia. Because puppies are still growing and have very small energy reserves, skipping meals is much more serious for them than it is for adult dogs. A puppy who stops eating may be experiencing anything from mild stress to a severe underlying infection.

Is This an Emergency?

Urgency Level: High. If a puppy under six months of age refuses food for 12 hours or more, it is considered a veterinary emergency. Puppies are highly prone to hypoglycemia (dangerously low blood sugar) and dehydration. These conditions can cause a rapid decline in health, leading to weakness, seizures, or collapse if not treated immediately by a professional.

How Photos Help Triage

Capturing a photo or video of your puppy can be incredibly helpful for a triage team. A photo of your puppy's gums can indicate their hydration and circulation levels—they should be pink and moist, not pale or tacky. Additionally, a video of your puppy's posture or any attempts to vomit helps the vet determine the severity of their distress before you even arrive at the clinic.

faqs.question: Can I wait until the morning to see if my puppy eats?
faqs.answer: No, if your puppy is very young or small-breed, waiting even a few hours can lead to life-threatening low blood sugar. It is best to call an emergency vet immediately.

faqs.question: What are the common causes of a puppy not eating?
faqs.answer: Common causes include intestinal parasites, viral infections like Parvovirus, dietary indiscretion, or physical obstructions. A veterinarian needs to perform diagnostics to find the exact cause.

faqs.question: Should I try to force-feed my puppy?
faqs.answer: Do not force-feed your puppy unless specifically instructed by a vet, as this can lead to aspiration pneumonia if the puppy is feeling nauseated or weak.
